cards.text: {{c1::Compound data types}} (also called composite data types) are types that are defined in terms of {{c2::other existing data types}}.

cards.front: What are the two main categories of data types in C++?
cards.back: Fundamental types and compound types. Every data type falls into one of these two categories as defined by the C++ standard.

cards.text: A {{c1::class type}} is a type that is a {{c2::struct}}, {{c3::class}}, or {{c4::union}}.

cards.front: Is a function a compound type or fundamental type?
cards.back: A function is a compound type. For example, a function void doSomething(int x, double y) has type void(int, double), which is composed of fundamental types.

cards.front: What kind of type is std::string?
cards.back: std::string is a class type (compound type), not a fundamental type.

cards.text: Pointer types include pointers to {{c1::objects}}, pointers to {{c2::functions}}, and pointers to {{c3::members}}.
Reference types in C++ include {{c1::lvalue}} references and {{c2::rvalue}} references.
